meaning: n.中心装饰品,(餐桌中央的摆饰)
examples: The reconstructed boat, as a symbol of the maritime connections that bound together the communities either side of the Channel, was the centerpiece.
He said that manufacturers of crisps and candies could play a central role in the Change4life campaign, the centerpiece of government efforts to boost healthy eating and fitness.
Gardens are the centerpiece of the pavilion.
Kerry has made the Israeli-Palestinian issue the centerpiece of his tenure as America's top diplomat.
The centerpiece of the home page is a collection showing covers of your recent reads.
